params.name: Riding Car
params.description:
On Nov 6, 1885 AD, Daimler installed the engine he developed on a wooden bicycle and successfully created the world's first motorcycle.
Most of the structure of this motorcycle, including the frame and the wheels, was made of wood and powered by a small gasoline engine mounted vertically in the frame. The engine drove the rear wheel through a belt and two-stage gear system. It did not have a suspension system, making for a rough ride, but it was a significant step forward in the development o …
